content:
# Final Review Proposal
**Producer:** agent:claude/claude-opus-4-6-max

## Summary

Comprehensive review of the failure reconciliation implementation. The core handler (failure_reconciliation.rs, ~1100 lines of logic + ~1500 lines of tests) is well-structured, follows the success_reconciliation patterns, and handles critical edge cases: idempotent replays, stale-attempt protection, same-run-id retries, and historical event repair. The controller state machine changes (Blocked preserving active context, new Blocked→Running/Reconciling transitions) are internally consistent. The CLI sync path correctly delegates failed runs to reconcile_failure_sync and early-returns before the success path (making the `unreachable!()` on the old Failed branch safe). The daemon integration in daemon_loop.rs gracefully falls back when reconciliation itself fails, enriching the error rather than masking it. The stale started_at rejection in fs.rs adds a necessary safety guard. The queries.rs refactoring to scope terminal events within attempt boundaries prevents stale run_failed events from being matched after resume. Test coverage is thorough across all acceptance criteria. No critical or high-severity bugs found.

## Amendments

None.
